findings.summary: Component counts, net count, and power rail detection are accurate; BAT1 and BAT2 classified as 'connector' instead of a battery/power type; Logo* graphic symbols classified as 'inductor' instead o...

correct.description: 68 total components, 213 nets, 21 power rails (including +/-12/24/36/48V, AC, Earth, GNDA, GND_CHASSIS, VBAT, VBUS, VCC) all correctly extracted. Component type breakdown matches the actual per-component types in the components list.

Y1 (Crystal) is correctly detected in signal_analysis.crystal_circuits. U1 (Generics:U_LDO_Fixed) is correctly detected as a power regulator with topology 'LDO'. OpAmp U2 (Generics:U_OPAMP) is detected as comparator_or_open_loop configuration.

This is a symbol library test sheet — all power rails are isolated single-symbol demos with no real drivers. The 20 pwr_flag_warnings are all valid for this context. The single_pin_nets list correctly flags isolated power symbols.

incorrect.description: BAT1 (Generics:BAT) and BAT2 (Generics:BAT_Multicell) are typed as 'connector' in both the components list and statistics. They are batteries and should be a distinct type (e.g., 'battery' or 'power'). The classification heuristic is matching the 'BAT' ref prefix to connector rather than battery.

Logo1–Logo5 (lib_ids: Logos:Logo_OpenHW, Logo_CE, Logo_RoHS, Logo_ESD, Logo_ISO_7010_W017) are all classified as type 'inductor'. These are silkscreen/graphic symbols with no electrical function. They should be excluded from meaningful analysis or tagged as 'graphic'/'logo'. Their inclusion inflates the inductor count from 1 real inductor (L1) to 6.

The BOM has only 3 entries. Entry 1 (value='NI', type='connector') groups BAT1, BAT2, C5, C8, L1, D1, FB1, R1-R4, Y1, F1 — 19 different component types all shown as 'connector'. Entry 3 (value='~', type='ic') groups diodes D3-D13, transistors Q1-Q4, transformers T1-T5, and OP1 — all shown as 'ic'. The 'type' field for each BOM row is taken from the first component in the group, making the BOM type column unreliable when components share the same value string.

findings.summary: Component count (8), type breakdown, and power rail detection are accurate; Net label tracing bug: LCD_TX/LCD_RX labels not resolved to J2 connector pins in KiCad 5 legacy format; Reset pin observa...

correct.description: 8 components (3 ic, 3 connector, 1 transistor, 1 resistor), power rails +3.3V/+5V/GND, and all footprints correctly extracted. BOM accurately lists all 8 unique parts with correct footprints and datasheets.

Q1 base=LCD_CTL, collector=GND, emitter=LCD_GND is an unusual but real topology (LCD backlight ground switch). The analyzer correctly identifies: type=bjt, load_type=connector (LCD connector on emitter side), base_resistors=[R1 1k]. The collector_is_power=true and emitter_is_ground=true flags are consistent with the actual net assignments.

The schematic has no capacitors at all; the WeMos module is powered directly from the IRM-03-5 without local decoupling. The design_observations entry for U2 correctly lists both rails as rails_without_caps.

incorrect.description: LCD_TX and LCD_RX net labels appear in the schematic connecting U2 (WeMos D1, D0) to J2 (RX, TX pins). The output shows LCD_TX and LCD_RX as single-device nets (only U2), while J2.RX and J2.TX land on anonymous nets __unnamed_9 and LCD_GND respectively. The KiCad 5 legacy parser is not correctly associating the text label coordinates with the wire endpoints at J2, causing the UART signals to appear disconnected from the display connector.

The design_observations entry for U2 (WeMos mini) RST pin says 'net': '+3.3V', 'has_pullup': false. RST is wired directly to the +3.3V rail, which constitutes a pull-up connection. The analyzer should detect a named power rail connected to a reset pin as a pull-up. Reporting false is incorrect and misleading.

missed.description: U1 is a MeanWell IRM-03-5, a 3W AC/DC module (5V output). U3 is a MeanWell SLC03-series isolated DC/DC converter. Neither appears in signal_analysis.power_regulators. The detector likely only recognizes regulators by lib_id keywords (LDO, VREG, etc.) and misses custom-library AC/DC and DC/DC modules. These are the primary power supply components of the design.
